title: wiki: bootstrap GitHub crawler eval corpus
body: Builds on the architecture decision in #42 and the bootstrap design in issue #18. Pre-builds the Lane D test corpus so once the crawler lands the snapshot tests are live. Reviewers wanted before merge.

title: wiki: ship typed-link extractor + persister
body: Lane B of the wiki bootstrap initiative. Implements zero-LLM-call link extraction parsing `[[type:slug]]` markdown and frontmatter scalars per the plan. Multi-reviewer because this touches the writer hook used by every crawler. cc [[person:richard]] [[person:maison]].

title: fix: typo in retrieval module docstring
body: One-line typo fix. No behavior change.

title: fix(auth): refresh OAuth tokens before 60s expiry window
body:
Fixes intermittent 401s during long-running ingestion drains. The auth service was refreshing tokens at expiry rather than ahead of expiry; under load the request would race the refresh and 401. Bumps the refresh window to 60s ahead of expiry and adds a regression test.

Doesn't introduce a new wiki page; this is a bug-fix on the existing auth service. Touches [[service_card:auth]].

title: decision: pick pgvector over Pinecone for vector store
body:
Closes #18.

Context: we evaluated pgvector (inside our existing Neon Postgres) against a managed Pinecone cluster for the Phase 0 retrieval stack.

Decision: pgvector wins.

Rationale:
- One less moving part (no separate vector DB to operate)
- Significantly lower monthly cost at our query volume
- Strong existing Postgres ops muscle memory on the team
- Hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense) is straightforward when both indexes live in the same database

Trade-offs accepted:
- pgvector lacks Pinecone's sharding-out-of-the-box; we'll revisit at >50M vectors
- IVFFlat tuning is on us

Reviewed and approved by [[person:maison]].

title: chore: bump httpx 0.27.2 -> 0.27.3
body: Routine dependency bump. No code changes.
